summary: Workflow has valid structure (pinned actions, appropriate permissions, correct shell specification) but SemanticVersionBumper.ps1 omits `Set-StrictMode -Version Latest` at module level—critical PowerShell best practice. Major issue: workflow test scenarios (lines 20–107) duplicate identical patterns 5+ times across patch/minor/major/JSON/changelog tests. Each repeats: `. ./SemanticVersionBumper.ps1`, temp dir setup, fixture copy, commit load, version bump, assertion. Should factor into a parameterized helper script or composite action to respect DRY. Regex patterns (BREAKING CHANGE detection, feat/fix matching) are undocumented magic values. Test harness (Run-ActTests.ps1) itself uses good helper functions to avoid duplication, but workflow doesn't apply same pattern.
